docs.title: Welcome
docs.text:
In the past years of my career I loooked at different Operations and Architecture scenarios.

On this journey I learned some standards in different detail grades.

This standards include, but not limit to

  • The Open Group Architecture Framework(TOGAF)
    • including linked publications like the TOGAF Leader's guide
  • arc42
  • ITIL 4
  • ...

These standards are often very open. In this documentation I share possible real world implementations while utilizing proven standards.

This is a collection to help in the establishment of an EA Capability or a Service Delivery Capability.

Besides processes and standards also practical tools from my point of view are referenced.

This won't be rocketscience, but is just my view on it!

docs.title: Collaboration
docs.text:
If you find a mistake or a missing citation, happily write me on LinkedIn or via Mastodon

You can also happily fork and add your own information. This documentation is currently hosted on GitHub

docs.title: Example for Mermaid
docs.text:
pie title Which animals do you prefer as pets?
    "Dogs" : 386
    "Cats" : 85
    "Rabbits" : 53
    "Hamsters" : 101

docs.title: Authentication/Authorization
docs.text: This should have some requirements fulfilled and some best practices.

docs.title: Client Communication
docs.text: In this documentation I will write about general communication to client and users.

docs.title: Reporting
docs.text:
Incidents can be reported from different ways. This affects the communication.

For example a client reported incident needs to have clear communication to the client.

Lots of incidents also are reported internally for example by Monitoring systems, etc...

docs.title: Flow
docs.text:
---
title: Incident communication Flow
---
flowchart LR
start@{shape: sm-circ }
classify["classifiy impact"]
client_affected@{label: "is the client affected?", shape: diam}
notify1["send notification of new incident with expected timeline and past occurences"]
solve["solving incident"]
is_solved@{label: "Is it solved?", shape: diam}
status_update["sending out a status update after a while to keep the client in the loop"]
doc_internal["Document Incident internal"]
stop@{shape: fr-circ}
resolve_notify["report incident is resolved"]
rca["send out Root Cause Analysis"]

start --> classify
classify --> client_affected
client_affected -->|yes| notify1
client_affected -->|no| doc_internal
doc_internal --> stop

notify1 --> solve
solve --> is_solved
is_solved -->|no| status_update
status_update --> solve
is_solved -->|yes| resolve_notify
resolve_notify --> rca
rca --> stop

docs.title: Information
docs.text:
The following information should be communicated:

  • What is the impact for the client?
    • is the application down or just degraded?
  • Is any data lost?
  • Is any data (potentially) accessible by unintended third parties?
  • Expected timeline
  • Were remediation actions for the future planned?
    • to give safety for the client

docs.title: Implemntation
docs.text:
To implement this some kind of Incident management must be implemented. In best case this supports the flow. The flow in the implemntation is going to be more complicated, as internal states also need to be communicated.

Also directly define Ownerships and Templates so it is clear who is responsible and in what format it should be send.
